what_it_does: Defines a right-side Vi chat rail for ConceptV Studio, with scoped suggested prompts, message history, model replies, image-generation routing, workshop edit-diff proposals, and inline actions for generated images or proposed edits.
observations: Uses React hooks through locally aliased globals rather than imports.
Seeds the chat with an HTML-formatted Vi greeting.
Maps canvas scopes to suggested prompts and human-readable labels.
Routes clear image-generation requests through window.cvOpenAI when Vi image generation is enabled.
Adds generated image outputs to a shared image store and provides adopt, navigation, and download controls.
In workshop scope, classifies edit intent and can render a WSDiffCard proposal before falling back to a text answer.
Builds a brand/system prompt inside the file and sends recent chat history to window.claude.complete.
Renders message HTML with dangerouslySetInnerHTML and handles data-nav links from message content.
standouts: Combines ordinary assistant chat, image generation, and editable workshop diffs in one rail component.
The system prompt embeds detailed ConceptV brand constraints, voice rules, and image-generation behavior.
Suggested prompt chips are scoped by design-system area and appear only in the initial state.
conventions: Uses global window services for AI, navigation, image storage, toast notifications, and workshop editing.
Uses dsa-* and cv-* class names for rail, message, composer, and image UI elements.
Keeps assistant replies narrow-rail friendly through prompt instructions and light markdown formatting.
Escapes generated assistant markdown in formatReply before allowing limited bold and code HTML.
Uses silent catch blocks for optional integrations where failure should not interrupt the chat path.
concerns: The onAction prop is accepted but unused in this file.
User-authored message text is rendered through dangerouslySetInnerHTML without passing through escapeHtml or formatReply.
Several window dependencies are assumed at render or call sites, including WSDiffCard, ViShape, window.claude, window.cvOpenAI, window.cvImageStore, window.WS_AI, and window.cvNav.
Silent catch blocks hide failure causes for image-store persistence and workshop classification/edit generation.
The workshop placeholder reads window.WS_AI bridge state during render, so placeholder text depends on mutable global state outside React state.
notes: This file is the persistent Vi assistant surface, acting as a scoped chat UI plus router for image generation and workshop edit proposals.
questions: Is onAction intended for future rail commands or should it be removed?
What guarantees that window.WSDiffCard and ViShape exist before ChatRail renders?
Should user messages be escaped before being rendered as HTML?
Should failed optional integrations surface diagnostic information somewhere outside the rail?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it creates a persistent AI conversation rail on the right, with added image and workshop-edit behavior.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make Vi a persistent design-system assistant that understands the current canvas, can answer in brand voice, can generate imagery when configured, and can propose direct edits in workshop contexts.
novelty: The file places assistant chat, brand-governed image generation, and reviewable document diffs behind one compact side-rail interaction model.
contribution: Contributes a scoped conversational rail that keeps design-system context close to every canvas, including suggested prompts, brand-aware AI prompting, image-output adoption, and editable workshop-diff review flows.
summary_for_embedding: ChatRail.jsx defines a global React Vi side-chat component for ConceptV Studio with scoped suggestions, model-backed replies, optional image generation, workshop edit proposals, and inline message actions.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for keeping an AI design-system assistant persistently available across canvases while routing specialized requests to image generation or editable workshop diffs when appropriate.

symbols.does: Renders and manages the persistent Vi chat rail UI.
symbols.description: ChatRail holds message, input, and thinking state, scrolls the message pane as content changes, selects suggested prompts by scope, and renders the rail header, transcript, composer, generated-image controls, and diff proposal cards. It coordinates user sends through the local send function and exposes scoped labels from SCOPE_LABEL.

symbols.does: Handles submitting a user message and producing the next rail response.
symbols.description: send trims input, appends the user message, blocks duplicate sends while thinking, then routes the request through image generation, workshop diff generation, or a text model completion. It also enriches image prompts with ConceptV brand style, persists generated images when possible, builds recent chat history for the model, and updates rail state for success or failure paths.

symbols.does: Applies a workshop diff proposal from a message.
symbols.description: applyDiffMessage looks up a message by index, exits if it has no proposal, and applies the proposal diff through window.WS_AI.bridge.apply. On success it changes the message to a diff-applied state and shows an undo-oriented toast; on failure it shows an apply-failed toast.

symbols.does: Marks a proposed workshop edit as discarded.
symbols.description: discardDiffMessage updates the indexed message in state to a diff-discarded kind and replaces its body text with a short discarded notice. It does not call the workshop bridge or mutate the active document.

symbols.does: Escapes and lightly formats assistant reply text for display as HTML.
symbols.description: formatReply converts incoming text to a string, escapes ampersands and angle brackets, converts markdown-style bold and inline code into b and code tags, and changes newlines to br tags. It is used for model replies before they are rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

symbols.does: Escapes basic HTML-sensitive characters in arbitrary text.
symbols.description: escapeHtml converts a value to a string and replaces ampersands, less-than signs, and greater-than signs with HTML entities. It is used when embedding error messages and proposal summaries into HTML-formatted rail messages.
